title: genista anglica subsp. ancistrocarpa
intro: Genista anglica subsp. ancistrocarpa, also known as the English broom or Scotch broom, is a plant species belonging to the legume family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and supporting the immune system. Genista anglica subsp. ancistrocarpa can be prepared as a herbal remedy in the form of teas, infusions, or tinctures, although its use should be approached with caution due to potential toxicity and interactions with certain medications.
